questions.question_text: Litware recently signed a contract to receive book reviews from an external provider. The provider exposes the reviews in Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) buckets. Litware must make the book reviews available in the lakehouse but must NOT store a duplicate copy of the data in OneLake. What should you implement?

Litware identifies the following data requirements: Process the SEO data in near-real-time (NRT). Make the book reviews available in the lakehouse without making a copy of the data. When a new book cover image arrives in the Files folder, process the image as soon as possible. You need to resolve the sales data issue while minimizing the amount of data transferred. What should you configure in the dataflow?

options.text: Split the dataflow into two dataflows.
Configure scheduled refresh for the dataflow.
Configure incremental refresh and set Store rows from the past to 1 month.
Configure incremental refresh and set Refresh rows from the past to 1 year.
Configure incremental refresh and set Refresh rows from the past to 1 month.

questions.question_text: You need to recommend a method to populate the POS1 data to the lakehouse medallion architecture layers. What should you recommend for each layer?

You must ensure that the use of email interaction data stored in an Amazon S3 bucket meets the following requirements: Minimize cross-cloud egress costs and prevent storing an additional copy of the raw data in the lakehouse. What should you do?

options.text: Create a workspace identity and enable high concurrency.
Create a shortcut and disable caching.
Create a workspace identity and use a data pipeline.
Create a shortcut and enable caching.

questions.question_text: You need to create the product dimension by using Apache Spark SQL. Products must include only active products and must exclude categories or subcategories that are not assigned to any product. How should you complete the query?

You must populate the MAR1 marketing application data in the bronze layer of Lakehouse1. Data from MAR1 is retrieved by calling REST API endpoints. The pipeline must process multiple entities in parallel. Which two activities should you include in the pipeline?

You have a Fabric warehouse named Warehouse1. Warehouse1 contains two tables: Employee(EmployeeID, EmployeeName, EmployeePosition) and Contract(EmployeeID, ContractType, StartDate, EndDate). You need to denormalize the tables so that the Employee table includes ContractType and StartDate values. The query must return the total number of employees per contract type but only for contract types that have more than two employees. How should you complete the SQL statement?
